forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KT9JV0N1RF0MMEBRZV4A6F3J
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: bf80
+project: command-events
+title: Publish commands/changed when the command registry changes (palette refresh)
+---
+Raise `notifications/commands/changed` when the set of registered commands changes (plugin load/unload, register/unregister), so the palette refreshes and plugins can react.
+
+Current state: the `notifications/commands/changed` constructor exists (notify.rs:279) but has NO publisher. The command service schedules a debounced `self.notifier.notify()` (service.rs:345) but never publishes this notification. The frontend availability cache already listens for a `commands/changed` epoch bump (use-command-availability.ts) — but nothing emits it.
+
+## Work
+- Publish `notifications/commands/changed` on the bridge from the command service when a command is registered/unregistered (and on plugin load/unload command churn). Hook the existing debounced notifier path (service.rs:345) to actually publish.
+- Confirm the frontend palette/availability already consumes the bridge-forwarded `notifications/commands/changed` (it expects it); wire if needed.
+- Declare on the command service tool (service.rs:285) via #[notification] struct=payload.
+- Coverage guard.
+
+## Acceptance
+Registering/unregistering a command publishes `notifications/commands/changed`; the palette refreshes; a plugin can `this.commands.on("changed", cb)`; declared == published.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,6 +1,18 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvwwnh43kb6b30gexhje2yvf
+  text: |-
+    Picked up. Research done. Findings:
+    - `notifications/commands/changed` constructor exists in notify.rs as bespoke `commands_changed(prov)` (thin: provenance only). The canonical struct==payload pattern (cf. CommandsExecuted) wants a #[notification] declared struct + from_declared, NOT a bespoke constructor.
+    - Command service already declares CommandsExecuted via #[notification] and feeds command_notifications() to operation_tool!. commands/changed is NOT yet in COMMAND_NOTIFICATIONS.
+    - ChangeNotifier (notifications.rs) already debounces register/unregister/purge and calls a pluggable Fn() emit. service.rs calls notifier.notify() on register/unregister and notify()+flush() on purge/unload. BUT bootstrap.rs builds via CommandService::new() which wires a NO-OP notifier sink — so production never publishes anything. This is the gap.
+    - Frontend: subscribeCommandsChanged (mcp-transport.ts) already listens for Tauri event "notifications/commands/changed"; the host per-window forwarder (commands.rs) re-broadcasts EVERY bridge notification by method verbatim (no allowlist). So publishing on the bridge reaches the frontend with ZERO frontend changes.
+
+    Plan: (1) declare CommandsChanged via #[notification] (thin, no fields) + add to COMMAND_NOTIFICATIONS + a publish helper from_declared; (2) add a NotifierSink seam + BridgeNotifierSink in bootstrap wired to host.notification_bridge(); wire the service notifier emit to call it; (3) coverage guard (declared==raised) + real-pipeline test. Frontend untouched. Payload: thin (provenance only) epoch bump — no command-set enrichment.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-24T13:21:15.907011+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KT9JV0N1RF0MMEBRZV4A6F3J
 position_column: doing

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -13,6 +13,28 @@

     Plan: (1) declare CommandsChanged via #[notification] (thin, no fields) + add to COMMAND_NOTIFICATIONS + a publish helper from_declared; (2) add a NotifierSink seam + BridgeNotifierSink in bootstrap wired to host.notification_bridge(); wire the service notifier emit to call it; (3) coverage guard (declared==raised) + real-pipeline test. Frontend untouched. Payload: thin (provenance only) epoch bump — no command-set enrichment.
   timestamp: 2026-06-24T13:21:15.907011+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvwxh8bk83sexr8facsze04r
+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed. Files changed:
+    - operations.rs: declared CommandsChanged via #[notification](method=notifications/commands/changed) — struct==payload, NO fields (thin epoch bump). Added commands_changed_notification() publish helper (from_declared) + added CommandsChanged to COMMAND_NOTIFICATIONS slice.
+    - txn.rs: new NotifierSink trait + NoopNotifierSink (mirrors ActionSink). Coverage guard now: raised_methods_equal_declared_methods (declared⟺raised over BOTH executed+changed) + commands_changed_is_a_thin_provenance_only_payload.
+    - service.rs: with_notifier_publish(SharedNotifierSink) builder rebuilds the ChangeNotifier so its debounced emit publishes commands_changed_notification() through the sink. (existing notify()/flush() on register/unregister/purge/unload now actually publish.)
+    - bootstrap.rs: BridgeNotifierSink (publishes on host.notification_bridge()); install_commands_module_with wires .with_notifier_publish(notifier_sink). THIS closes the production gap (was no-op).
+    - lib.rs: exported NotifierSink/NoopNotifierSink/SharedNotifierSink + commands_changed_notification.
+    - meta_tree.rs: updated to assert BOTH executed+changed declared; changed leaf has 0 domain params.
+    - notify.rs (plugin crate): removed the bespoke McpNotification::commands_changed(prov) constructor (duplicate wire-method literal in a non-owning crate) — replaced with a doc note; its only caller (a bridge-forwarding test) now builds the notification inline by method.
+
+    Real-pipeline test: tests/integration/commands_changed_e2e.rs (4 tests) drives register/unregister verb → ChangeNotifier (debounced) → bootstrap-wired BridgeNotifierSink → bridge → subscriber. Covers: single register publishes exactly 1; 5 rapid registers coalesce to 1; unregister publishes 1; no-op unregister publishes 0.
+
+    Frontend: NO changes needed. subscribeCommandsChanged (mcp-transport.ts) already listens for Tauri event "notifications/commands/changed"; the host per-window forwarder re-broadcasts every bridge notification by method verbatim. use-command-availability.ts already epoch-bumps on it.
+
+    Payload choice: thin/empty (provenance-only). The consumer treats it as "registry changed, refetch" — no command-set enrichment (no enrichment re-fetch). Documented on the CommandsChanged struct.
+
+    Stale refs corrected (2026-06-04 desc): notify.rs:279 → the constructor was at ~292 (now removed); service.rs:345 notifier path → the debounced notify()/flush() calls are throughout handle_register/handle_unregister/purge_caller/install_unload_hook_for; service.rs:285 declaration belongs in operations.rs (build_tool_definition already passed command_notifications()). GOTCHA hit: stale rlibs (sccache + tauri dev) — touch + rerun fixed.
+
+    Verification: cargo nextest -p swissarmyhammer-command-service = 173 passed. -p kanban-app -E 'not test(ai_panel_e2e)' = 191 passed. event_subscription_e2e = 6 passed. cargo fmt clean. clippy clean on touched files (pre-existing warnings in caption.rs/builtin_nav_commands_e2e unrelated). Pre-existing fails disregarded: file_notes_e2e, example_layering_e2e (CWD-isolation); hot_reload_e2e::class_field... passes in isolation (parallel artifact).
+  timestamp: 2026-06-24T13:36:24.435229+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KT9JV0N1RF0MMEBRZV4A6F3J
 position_column: doing

 ## Acceptance

+Registering/unregistering a command publishes `notifications/commands/changed`; the palette refreshes; a plugin can `this.commands.on("changed", cb)`; declared == published.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-24 07:40)
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/notify.rs:474` — Hardcoded polling iteration count (50) should be a named constant. This configures retry behavior in the test—when combined with the sleep interval, it sets a total timeout. Retry limits and timeouts are explicit magic numbers that should not be inlined. Extract as: const FORWARDER_DRAIN_POLL_ITERATIONS: usize = 50;.
\ No newline at end of file

Extract as: const FORWARDER_DRAIN_POLL_ITERATIONS: usize = 50;.

All load-bearing items passed: the production gap closure is real (`install_commands_module_with` in bootstrap.rs now chains the real `BridgeNotifierSink` instead of the no-op default — the engine probed this and refuted it as a problem), declared==raised bidirectional coverage guard is genuine, the debounce-coalescing real-pipeline test (`commands_changed_e2e.rs`: 1 register→1, 5 rapid→1, unregister→1, no-op→0) is real, and the redundant `McpNotification::commands_changed` constructor removal leaves exactly one wire-method source.

+
+    Payload: thin/empty (provenance-only) epoch-bump — the frontend `use-command-availability.ts` re-reads the registry itself (no enrichment re-fetch). Frontend needed no changes (already consumed the bridge-forwarded notification). Moving to done.
